mappings.rationaleAI: Directly addresses the central nervous system (brain and spinal cord, J.1), the peripheral nervous system with sensory and motor neurons (J.2), and the autonomic subdivision regulating heartbeat, digestion, and breathing (J.3), along with neurotransmitter-mediated synaptic communication throughout the nervous system (J).

Examines how the brain integrates data from vision (J.4), hearing (J.5), touch (J.6.a), taste (J.6.d), and smell (J.6.e), including cross-modal sensory interactions and multisensory integration in flavor perception combining olfactory and gustatory inputs.

Focuses on the heart as a biological organ (A.1) and Harvey's seventeenth-century discovery of blood circulation revealing the heart as a mechanical pump driving systemic and pulmonary circulation (A.4).

Directly covers the endocrine system and more than eighty known hormones including insulin (E.1.e), cortisol and adrenalin (E.1.b), and oestrogen and testosterone (E.2.a), produced in glands throughout the body, with hormonal roles in homeostasis, metabolism, growth, and reproduction via feedback loops involving the hypothalamus (E.1.d) and pituitary (E.1.c).

Examines blood's scientific significance including Galen's characterization of blood as containing the force of life, the development of blood transfusion, and how medical progress has transformed understanding of blood's properties and components (A.3).

Traces understanding of the human brain's structure from Hippocrates identifying it as the source of thought, through Renaissance anatomists, to modern neuroimaging revealing functional architecture in real time, directly relevant to the central nervous system (J.1).

Covers brain science from nineteenth-century discoveries of speech localization to modern neuroimaging, illuminating the structure and functional organization of the central nervous system (J.1) and biological mechanisms of neural processing (J).
Addresses physiological mechanisms of pain signaling, neural pathways for nociception, and brain processing of pain data, directly relevant to the cutaneous sense of pain (J.6.a).

The internal body clock is governed by the hypothalamus (E.1.d) and involves the pineal gland's secretion of melatonin (E.1.h), connecting endocrine regulation to daily cycles of physiological change in response to light and darkness.

Covers respiratory lung mechanics including buccal pumping and bellows ventilation, and the functional anatomy of lungs (C.2), with discussion of how musculature was repurposed to regulate breathing dynamics (C.3), providing comparative context for human respiratory structures.

Enzymes as biological catalysts control the speed of chemical reactions in living organisms, including breaking down and building molecules fundamental to the digestive process (D.2) and cellular regulation throughout the body.
